brief.headline_zh: 欧盟商标司法收紧与融资新政
brief.headline_en: EU Trademark Law Tightens While IP Financing Opportunities Expand
brief.summary_zh: 2026年Q1-Q2，欧盟商标司法趋严：CJEU裁定异议权利须全程有效，普通法院强化声誉评估。同时，恶意抢注风险激增，动态商标因技术功能被拒。政策面利好中小企业，EUIPO基金补贴及IP融资报告释放5800亿欧元潜力。企业需平衡合规成本与资产化机遇。
key_points: 司法裁判趋严：CJEU明确异议期间在先权利必须持续有效，普通法院强调商标声誉需全局评估，动态商标若仅具技术功能将被驳回。
注册风险升级：德国等地恶意利用AI抢注未注册标志现象激增，且假冒碳粉等耗材危害引发UKIPO警示，供应链合规压力加大。
政策红利释放：EUIPO推出2026中小企业基金降低申请成本，并发布报告建议通过IP抵押释放5800亿欧元创新融资，助力品牌资产化。
actions: 优化注册策略：利用EUIPO SME Fund补贴申请欧盟商标以覆盖27国，建立全球监控机制防范AI恶意抢注，确保异议权利全程稳定。
强化合规风控：排查出口产品是否侵犯如利摩日瓷器等新获保护的地理标志，建立供应商溯源体系以应对假冒耗材指控。
探索资产融资：规范知识产权管理流程，评估将商标作为抵押物获取融资的可行性，把握欧盟IP金融化政策窗口期。
watchlist: 关注欧洲法院后续是否就‘纯技术功能’动态商标及声誉评估给出更明确的配套裁判口径。
监测塞浦路斯等成员国在数字时代下对商标战略资产属性的具体规则动态及配套安排。
追踪欧盟关于IP-backed finance政策落地的具体细则，以及中国特产申请欧盟GI的合规路径进展。
brief.summary_en: During the first half of 2026, EU trademark jurisprudence has tightened significantly. The Court of Justice ruled that earlier rights must remain valid throughout opposition proceedings, while the General Court emphasized comprehensive reputation assessments and rejected motion marks lacking distinctiveness. Concurrently, risks from AI-driven bad faith filings and counterfeit supply chains are escalating. On the policy front, the EUIPO launched its SME Fund to subsidize registrations and released a report highlighting €580 billion in potential IP-backed financing. Companies must balance stricter compliance requirements with emerging opportunities to leverage intellectual property as collateral for innovation funding.
key_points_en: Judicial standards have hardened: The CJEU mandates continuous validity of earlier rights during oppositions, the General Court requires global reputation assessment, and purely fu...
Registration risks are rising due to AI-enabled bad faith filings in jurisdictions like Germany and severe operational threats from counterfeit consumables, increasing supply chain...
Policy incentives support assetization: The EUIPO SME Fund reduces application costs, while new frameworks aim to unlock substantial capital by treating trademarks as viable collat...
actions_en: Optimize registration strategies by utilizing EUIPO subsidies for unitary EU coverage, establishing global monitoring against AI squatting, and ensuring prior rights remain enforce...
Strengthen compliance controls by auditing export products against newly protected geographical indications and implementing rigorous supplier traceability to mitigate counterfeit...
Explore asset-based financing by standardizing IP management processes and evaluating the feasibility of using registered trademarks as collateral under evolving EU financial regul...
watchlist_en: Monitor subsequent case law clarifying the threshold for 'purely technical' motion marks and the scope of global reputation assessments.
Track national implementations regarding the strategic asset status of trademarks in digital contexts, particularly in member states like Cyprus.
Follow the finalization of EU policies on IP-backed finance and the regulatory pathways for non-EU geographical indications seeking EU protection.

items.title: Judgment of the General Court in Case T-24/25 Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO - Works 11 Michał Lubiński (Obelix)
items.title_zh: 普通法院撤销EUIPO决定：武器类“Obelix”商标应无效
items.title_en: General Court Annuls EUIPO Decision: Obelix Mark for Weapons Must Be Invalidated
items.summary: The General Court nullifies EUIPO’s refusal to invalidate the word mark Obelix for weapons, ammunition and explosives
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中撤销EUIPO拒绝宣告“Obelix”武器商标无效的决定。出版商Les Éditions Albert René依据在先OBELIX商标及声誉受损申请无效，EUIPO曾以声誉证据不足为由驳回。法院指出EUIPO分析有误，未综合评估商标声誉及两商标间的联系。法院强调需全局考察在先商标显著性及公众联想，认定存在损害在先商标声誉的风险，支持了权利人的主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The General Court of the European Union annulled an EUIPO decision refusing to invalidate the word mark OBELIX for weapons, ammunition, and explosives. Les Éditions Albert René, publisher of the Astérix & Obélix series, sought invalidation based on its earlier EU trade mark OBELIX and alleged damage to that mark’s reputation. The EUIPO had previously rejected the application, ruling that proof of the earlier mark’s reputation was insufficient. The General Court found the EUIPO’s analysis incomplete and erroneous, noting it failed to properly consider evidence of use with the ® symbol and in combination with the Asterix sign. The Court emphasized that reputation must be assessed globally, considering all relevant factors. It concluded that the public could associate the marks, creating a risk of unfair advantage or detriment to the distinctive character or repute of the earlier mark, thereby supporting the rights holder’s claim for invalidation.
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the protection of well-known cultural signs against cross-class registration, particularly when distinctiveness and reputation are established through consistent use. Rights holders should maintain robust global monitoring systems to detect potentially infringing applications early. Crucially, parties must accumulate comprehensive evidence of trademark use, including co-branding instances and registered status indicators, to substantiate reputation claims. In opposition or invalidation proceedings, demonstrating the likelihood of public association remains vital, even across disparate product categories, to successfully argue against unfair exploitation of a mark’s goodwill.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定EUIPO对在先商标声誉评估不完整，忽视关键使用证据", "强调需全局评估商标间联系，不能仅因商品差异大而否定关联", "撤销EUIPO决定，支持基于声誉受损宣告争议商标无效的诉求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court ruled the EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation was incomplete, ignoring key evidence of commercial use and registration symbols.", "Global assessment is required for trademark links; significant differences in goods alone do not preclude public association or reputational damage.", "The General Court annulled the refusal, upholding the invalidation request based on the risk of detriment to the reputation of the well-known earlier mark."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Well-known brands face lower thresholds for proving cross-class confusion when reputation is clearly evidenced by market presence.", "Practice note: Preserve detailed records of trademark usage, including ® markings and joint branding, to substantiate acquired distinctiveness and reputation.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in assessing 'link' between marks across non-competing goods, especially involving iconic cultural characters."]
core_points_en: The Court ruled the EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation was incomplete, ignoring key evidence of commercial use and registration symbols.
Global assessment is required for trademark links; significant differences in goods alone do not preclude public association or reputational damage.
The General Court annulled the refusal, upholding the invalidation request based on the risk of detriment to the reputation of the well-known earlier mark.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Well-known brands face lower thresholds for proving cross-class confusion when reputation is clearly evidenced by market presence.
Practice note: Preserve detailed records of trademark usage, including ® markings and joint branding, to substantiate acquired distinctiveness and reputation.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in assessing 'link' between marks across non-competing goods, especially involving iconic cultural characters.

items.title: Fake toner, real trouble: protect your printers from counterfeits
items.title_zh: UKIPO警示：假冒碳粉危害大，需强化防伪保护
items.title_en: UKIPO Warns of Serious Risks from Counterfeit Toner and Ink Cartridges
items.summary: Counterfeit ink and toner cartridges can be poor quality and can cause serious problems, especially for small businesses.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布博客文章，警示假冒墨盒和碳粉对中小企业造成的严重损害。文章指出全球假冒贸易规模巨大且常关联有组织犯罪，电商平台第三方卖家易成售假渠道。UKIPO联合ICCE等机构开展执法与情报协调，强调造假者不承担赔偿责任。建议企业建立严格采购体系，通过比对正品特征识别假货，以规避法律与经济损失风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has issued a warning regarding the significant harms caused by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly affecting small businesses. The blog highlights that while often perceived as low-risk, fake print consumables can cause severe operational damage. Global trade in counterfeit goods is estimated at USD 467 billion, with strong links to organized crime. Online marketplaces facilitate the sale of these items through third-party sellers offering tempting prices. Crucially, counterfeiters cannot be held liable for damages and offer no compensation. The UKIPO collaborates with the Imaging Consumables Coalition of Europe, Middle East and Africa (ICCE) to coordinate enforcement and intelligence efforts against counterfeiting networks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国打印耗材出口企业应警惕被卷入假冒供应链指控。需建立严格的知识产权合规与溯源体系，主动配合品牌方打假。同时，进口商应加强供应商尽职调查，避免采购来源不明的低价耗材，以防因销售假冒产品面临UKIPO执法行动及民事索赔风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating within European supply chains should prioritize robust intellectual property compliance and traceability mechanisms. Exporters must ensure their distribution channels are not inadvertently used for counterfeit goods, while importers need rigorous supplier due diligence to avoid sourcing unverified, low-cost alternatives. Failure to verify product origins may result in enforcement actions by authorities like the UKIPO and exposure to civil liability claims. Proactive cooperation with brand owners and industry coalitions is essential to mitigate legal risks and protect commercial reputation against allegations of facilitating counterfeit trade.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO警示假冒耗材致损严重，造假者免责且不赔偿", "全球假冒贸易达4670亿美元，常关联有组织犯罪网络", "建议企业完善采购系统，比对正品特征以识别假冒商品"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Counterfeit print consumables cause serious business damage, yet manufacturers bear no liability or compensation responsibility.", "Global counterfeit trade reaches approximately USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks.", "Businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需建立合规溯源体系，防范假冒供应链指控", "进口商应加强供应商尽调，避免采购不明来源低价耗材"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish strict IP compliance and traceability systems to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains.", "Practice note: Conduct thorough supplier due diligence to avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumables.", "What to monitor: Enforcement trends by UKIPO and ICCE regarding counterfeit print materials and online marketplace liabilities."]
core_points_en: Counterfeit print consumables cause serious business damage, yet manufacturers bear no liability or compensation responsibility.
Global counterfeit trade reaches approximately USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks.
Businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish strict IP compliance and traceability systems to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains.
Practice note: Conduct thorough supplier due diligence to avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumables.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends by UKIPO and ICCE regarding counterfeit print materials and online marketplace liabilities.

items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
items.title_en: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
items.summary: Discover how the concept of ‘Bösgläubigkeit’ (bad faith) operates in German law and how to avoid filing or being infringed by bad‑faith filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击", "德国商标法规定，无真实使用意图且旨在滥用权利的注册可被驳回或在事后宣告无效", "恶意认定需结合主观意图与客观环境综合评估，德国司法实践深受欧盟法院判例影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["尽早完成德国商标注册并建立监控机制，防范恶意抢注带来的电商下架风险", "遭遇恶意指控时，应积极收集在先使用证据，依法提起无效宣告以维权"]

items.title: Cyprus Trademarks - Protecting Brand Value In A Digital & Investor World
items.title_zh: 塞浦路斯商标：数字与投资时代的品牌价值保护
items.title_en: Cyprus Trademarks: Safeguarding Brand Value in Digital and Investment Contexts
items.summary: A strong brand is one of the greatest assets a business can build. Yet many Cyprus companies overlook trademarks, treating the registration of their brand name or logo, as a bureaucratic formality rather than a strategic decision. This can lead to lost market share, investor hesitation, and costly disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出塞浦路斯企业常忽视商标注册的战略价值，将其视为官僚程序，导致市场份额流失和投资受阻。作者强调商标是创造竞争优势的法律工具，能防止竞争对手搭便车。内容涵盖商标定义、注册路径（塞浦路斯、欧盟及国际）、常见误区及内部准备检查。旨在指导企业通过正确管理商标资产，实现业务增长、吸引投资并最大化品牌价值。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis highlights that many Cyprus-based enterprises frequently underestimate the strategic importance of trademark registration, mistakenly treating it as a mere bureaucratic formality. This oversight often results in significant market share erosion, investor hesitation, and expensive legal disputes. The article emphasizes that trademarks function as critical legal instruments establishing competitive advantages and preventing competitors from capitalizing on brand reputation. Although intangible, properly registered trademarks deliver tangible business value by serving as defensive shields against infringement and enabling growth, investment attraction, and market expansion. The guide provides comprehensive insights into trademark definitions, strategic registration pathways across Cyprus, the EU, and internationally, alongside common pitfalls to avoid. It aims to help businesses maximize brand value through effective management and proactive protection strategies.
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating in Europe should shift focus from purely sales-driven strategies to prioritizing intellectual property as a fundamental corporate asset. Mere formal compliance is insufficient; a robust, layered protection strategy combining national and EU-wide registrations is essential. In digital markets, preemptive defensive registrations are crucial to mitigate risks of brand squatting or rights defects. Such vulnerabilities can severely hinder financing opportunities and lead to prohibitive enforcement costs. Proactive trademark management ensures brand security during international expansion, supports investor confidence, and safeguards long-term commercial interests against competitive encroachment and legal challenges.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["纠正塞企将商标注册视为单纯官僚程序的误区，强调其战略资产属性", "解析商标作为法律锚点，在区分来源、防止侵权及提升商业价值中的作用", "提供从定义、注册路径到避免常见误区的全面指南，助力品牌增值"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Corrects the misconception that trademark registration is merely administrative, emphasizing its role as a core strategic asset for business valuation.", "Explains how trademarks act as legal anchors distinguishing commercial origin, preventing infringement, and enhancing overall business worth.", "Offers a practical guide covering definitions, multi-jurisdictional registration paths, and methods to avoid common errors while maximizing brand value."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat trademarks as core assets rather than routine compliance tasks to secure investment readiness and market position.", "Practice note: Implement combined national and EU registration strategies to build a comprehensive, multi-layered protective network.", "What to monitor: Preemptively register defensively in digital channels to prevent brand squatting and avoid costly rights disputes."]
core_points_en: Corrects the misconception that trademark registration is merely administrative, emphasizing its role as a core strategic asset for business valuation.
Explains how trademarks act as legal anchors distinguishing commercial origin, preventing infringement, and enhancing overall business worth.
Offers a practical guide covering definitions, multi-jurisdictional registration paths, and methods to avoid common errors while maximizing brand value.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat trademarks as core assets rather than routine compliance tasks to secure investment readiness and market position.
Practice note: Implement combined national and EU registration strategies to build a comprehensive, multi-layered protective network.
What to monitor: Preemptively register defensively in digital channels to prevent brand squatting and avoid costly rights disputes.

items.title: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reduce Trademark Costs For SMEs
items.title_zh: EUIPO 2026 中小企业基金：降低商标申请成本
items.title_en: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reducing Trademark Registration Costs for Eligible Enterprises
items.summary: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (“EUIPO”) SME Fund 2026 enables eligible small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to reclaim part of the cost of trademark registration...
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO 将于 2026 年 2 月启动中小企业基金，符合条件的企业可报销部分国家及欧盟商标注册费。该举措旨在应对日益激烈的品牌竞争，鼓励中小企业尽早布局知识产权。文章对比了马耳他国内商标与欧盟商标的覆盖范围，强调早期申请对规避冲突及获取全域保护的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) will launch the SME Fund in February 2026, enabling eligible small and medium-sized enterprises to reclaim a portion of their trademark registration fees. This initiative covers costs for both national applications within Member States and European Union Trademarks (EUTM). The program aims to mitigate rising brand competition by encouraging early intellectual property protection strategies. By offering financial reimbursement, the EUIPO supports businesses in securing their brand identity without significant upfront expenditure. The analysis highlights the strategic advantage of EUTMs, which provide immediate coverage across all twenty-seven EU Member States, contrasting this with single-country filings. Early application is emphasized as a critical measure to avoid potential conflicts, reduce opposition risks, and ensure comprehensive legal protection for brand assets throughout the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国赴欧中小企业应密切关注该基金申请窗口，利用财政补贴降低欧盟商标布局成本。建议结合市场规划，优先选择欧盟商标以实现广域保护，避免因费用顾虑延迟注册而遭遇抢注或侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should evaluate the EUIPO SME Fund as a viable mechanism to lower the financial barriers associated with European brand protection. Leveraging these subsidies allows for more robust IP portfolio management without straining operational budgets. Companies must strategically weigh the cost-efficiency of unitary EU trademarks against individual national registrations to align with their expansion goals. Delaying registration due to cost concerns increases exposure to bad-faith filings and enforcement challenges. Proactive planning ensures that brand assets are secured before market entry, providing a stronger legal foundation for commercial activities and dispute resolution within the EU jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["eligible 中小企业可获商标申请费用部分报销，涵盖欧盟及成员国层面", "欧盟商标单次申请即覆盖 27 国，相比单一国家注册更具扩张优势", "提前布局商标能有效降低异议风险，确立品牌独占权并防止侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Eligible SMEs can receive partial reimbursement for trademark application fees at both EU and national member state levels.", "A single European Union Trademark application provides immediate protection across all twenty-seven EU Member States.", "Early trademark filing significantly reduces opposition risks and establishes exclusive rights to prevent infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 EUIPO 基金补贴，显著降低中国品牌进入欧盟市场的知识产权门槛", "权衡单一国与欧盟商标差异，制定符合扩张战略的高性价比注册方案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize EUIPO subsidies to significantly lower the financial threshold for establishing trademark protection in the European market.", "Practice note: Assess the strategic benefits of unitary EU trademarks versus national filings to create a cost-effective registration roadmap.", "What to monitor: Track the specific application windows and eligibility criteria for the 2026 SME Fund to secure timely reimbursements."]
core_points_en: Eligible SMEs can receive partial reimbursement for trademark application fees at both EU and national member state levels.
A single European Union Trademark application provides immediate protection across all twenty-seven EU Member States.
Early trademark filing significantly reduces opposition risks and establishes exclusive rights to prevent infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize EUIPO subsidies to significantly lower the financial threshold for establishing trademark protection in the European market.
Practice note: Assess the strategic benefits of unitary EU trademarks versus national filings to create a cost-effective registration roadmap.
What to monitor: Track the specific application windows and eligibility criteria for the 2026 SME Fund to secure timely reimbursements.

items.title: La porcelaine de limoges
items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器获欧盟首项手工业地理标志保护
items.title_en: Limoges Porcelain Becomes First EU Industrial and Artisanal Geographical Indication
items.summary: La porcelaine de limoges sgurtler@inpi.fr 11 mai 2026 Avec l'entrée en vigueur du règlement européen sur les indications géographiques pour les produits artisanaux et industriels le 1er décembre 2025, la porcelaine de Limoges devient ce 15 mai la première indication géographique protégée (IGP) française artisanale et industrielle à être enregistrée au niveau européen auprès de l'Office de l'Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO). Cette reconnaissance marque une étape décisive
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月15日，利摩日瓷器成为欧盟《手工业和工业产品地理标志条例》生效后，首个在欧盟知识产权局注册的法国手工业地理标志。该认证基于其自2017年起获得的法国国家认可，通过简化程序转化而来。此举赋予其在欧盟27国的排他性保护及官方标识使用权，标志着欧盟对传统工艺知识产权保护的实质性落地，为其他法国手工艺品进入欧盟保护体系开辟路径。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 15, 2026, Limoges porcelain was registered as the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication at the European Union Intellectual Property Office. This registration follows the entry into force of the new EU regulation on geographical indications for craft and industrial products on December 1, 2025. Leveraging its existing national recognition granted by the French National Institute of Industrial Property in 2017, Limoges porcelain benefited from a simplified transformation procedure without an opposition phase. The decision grants exclusive protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and authorizes the use of the official protected geographical indication logo. This milestone demonstrates the operationalization of the new EU framework, setting a precedent for protecting traditional craftsmanship and potentially paving the way for other French artisanal sectors to secure similar pan-European intellectual property rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟手工业GI保护机制正式运行，中国企业需警惕出口产品因名称或产地描述不当引发的侵权风险。建议排查对欧出口的传统工艺品，避免使用受保护的地理名称。同时，关注欧盟新规则下中国优质特产申请欧盟GI的可能性，利用双边协定提前布局品牌护城河，规避合规陷阱。
items.ai_insight_en: The activation of the EU geographical indication regime for craft and industrial products establishes a new layer of intellectual property protection that businesses exporting to Europe must consider. Companies should audit their product naming conventions and marketing materials to ensure they do not infringe upon newly registered geographical names or imply false origins. Simultaneously, this development highlights potential pathways for non-EU producers to seek protection under bilateral agreements or future regulatory expansions. Monitoring the evolution of this registry is essential for strategic brand positioning and compliance, helping enterprises avoid legal pitfalls while identifying opportunities to leverage geographical authenticity in the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利摩日瓷器获欧盟首项手工业IGP注册", "依托法国国家GI经简化程序转化获批", "覆盖欧盟27国并启用官方保护标识"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication registered under the new EU regulatory framework.", "Registration utilized a simplified transformation process based on prior national recognition by the French National Institute of Industrial Property.", "The designation provides exclusive protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and permits use of the official geographical indication logo."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["排查对欧出口品名避免GI侵权风险", "关注中国特产申请欧盟GI的合规机遇"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Audit export product names and descriptions to prevent infringement risks associated with newly registered EU geographical indications.", "Practice note: Evaluate eligibility for EU geographical indication protection under applicable bilateral agreements to strengthen brand defensibility.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent registrations and procedural developments in the EU craft and industrial geographical indication registry."]
core_points_en: Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication registered under the new EU regulatory framework.
Registration utilized a simplified transformation process based on prior national recognition by the French National Institute of Industrial Property.
The designation provides exclusive protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and permits use of the official geographical indication logo.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Audit export product names and descriptions to prevent infringement risks associated with newly registered EU geographical indications.
Practice note: Evaluate eligibility for EU geographical indication protection under applicable bilateral agreements to strengthen brand defensibility.
What to monitor: Track subsequent registrations and procedural developments in the EU craft and industrial geographical indication registry.

items.title: Wanneer wordt een bewegingsmerk geweigerd wegens technische functionaliteit?
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定：纯技术功能动态商标将被驳回
items.title_en: EU General Court Rejects Motion Mark for Hinged Window Due to Technical Functionality
items.summary: In January 2026, the General Court of the EU refused the application for a motion mark of a hinged window. The General Court considers that the movement consists only of a technical functional process.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 1 月，欧盟普通法院维持 EUIPO 决定，驳回德国 Kct 公司关于折叠窗开合动画的动态商标注册申请。法院认定该运动序列仅由技术功能过程组成，缺乏显著性且属于《欧盟商标条例》禁止注册的技术解决方案范畴，旨在防止通过商标权无限期垄断技术特征。
items.ai_summary_en: In January 2026, the General Court of the European Union upheld the EUIPO’s decision to refuse registration of a motion mark filed by German company Kct GmbH & Co. The application depicted an animation of a hinged window opening and closing. The Court determined that this movement sequence consisted exclusively of technical functional processes, thereby lacking inherent distinctiveness. Crucially, the mark fell within the scope of Article 7(1)(e)(ii) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation, which prohibits the registration of signs consisting solely of shapes or characteristics necessary to obtain a technical result. This legal provision aims to prevent companies from securing indefinite monopolies on technical solutions through trademark law, effectively extending the protection period beyond that of patents. Consequently, the Court confirmed that such purely functional animations cannot be registered as trademarks.
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants seeking to register motion marks in the EU must ensure their submissions transcend mere descriptions of technical mechanisms. Purely functional animations depicting standard operational movements are likely to face refusal based on technical functionality grounds. To enhance distinctiveness and mitigate rejection risks, it is advisable to incorporate unique, non-functional visual elements into the design. These may include specific color combinations, artistic stylizations, or distinctive deformations that do not serve a technical purpose. By integrating such aesthetic features, brands can better demonstrate that the mark serves as an indicator of origin rather than just a technical process, aligning with current judicial interpretations regarding the separation of function and brand identity.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["动态商标若仅展示产品技术运作过程，将被视为缺乏显著性", "欧盟法律禁止注册仅为获取技术结果所必需的形状或特征", "法院强调需区分功能性要素与装饰性要素以判定可注册性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Motion marks displaying only product technical operations are deemed to lack distinctiveness under EU trademark law.", "EU legislation explicitly bans registering shapes or features strictly necessary to achieve a specific technical result.", "Courts emphasize distinguishing between functional elements and decorative aspects when assessing registrability."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid filing motion marks that merely describe standard product technical workflows to prevent automatic rejection.", "Practice note: Integrate non-functional decorative elements into animation designs to strengthen trademark distinctiveness.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in distinguishing technical functionality from aesthetic branding in motion mark cases."]
core_points_en: Motion marks displaying only product technical operations are deemed to lack distinctiveness under EU trademark law.
EU legislation explicitly bans registering shapes or features strictly necessary to achieve a specific technical result.
Courts emphasize distinguishing between functional elements and decorative aspects when assessing registrability.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid filing motion marks that merely describe standard product technical workflows to prevent automatic rejection.
Practice note: Integrate non-functional decorative elements into animation designs to strengthen trademark distinctiveness.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in distinguishing technical functionality from aesthetic branding in motion mark cases.

items.title: Going nowhere with a bathing ape: earlier rights in EUTM oppositions
items.title_zh: CJEU裁定：欧盟商标异议中在先权利须在决定时持续有效
items.title_en: CJEU Ruling: Earlier Rights Must Remain Valid at EUIPO Decision in EU Trade Mark Oppositions
items.summary: A Bathing Ape (BAPE) is a famous, luxury, Japanese fashion brand. The eponymous BAPE is sometimes stern and sometimes very cute. The cute BAPE is Baby…
items.ai_summary_zh: 日本品牌BAPE因英国未注册权在异议期间因脱欧失效，遭EUIPO驳回。CJEU在C-337/22 P案中裁定，主张在先权利不仅需在争议商标申请日存在，还必须在EUIPO作出最终决定时保持有效且可执行。若权利在程序中灭失，异议即失败。此判决澄清了法律不确定性，恢复了EUIPO的既有做法，强调权利在整个诉讼期间的稳定性至关重要。
items.ai_summary_en: The Japanese brand BAPE faced dismissal of its EU trade mark opposition after UK unregistered rights became ineffective due to Brexit during proceedings. In Case C-337/22 P, the Court of Justice of the European Union ruled that relying on earlier rights requires them to exist not only at the contested application’s filing date but also to remain valid and enforceable when the EUIPO issues its final decision. If these rights lapse during proceedings, the opposition fails. This judgment resolves legal uncertainty stemming from conflicting EUIPO and appellate court decisions, restoring the established approach that emphasizes the continuous stability of rights throughout the entire litigation period.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权时，应避免仅依赖易失效的短期或未注册权利。建议优先选择稳定的注册商标作为异议基础，并建立动态监控机制，确保在漫长的异议及上诉期内，在先权利持续有效，防止因权利瑕疵导致维权失败。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly impacts prosecution strategy by extending risk exposure for opponents throughout lengthy opposition and appeal phases. Practitioners should prioritize stable registered trade marks over short-term or unregistered rights to mitigate validity risks. Establishing dynamic monitoring mechanisms is essential to ensure continuous compliance with renewal deadlines and use requirements. Failure to maintain the underlying right can prove fatal to an opposition, making proactive portfolio management critical for successful enforcement in the EU jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定在先权利须在EUIPO最终决定时仍有效且可执行", "异议期间权利若灭失将直接导致异议失败，风险贯穿全程", "权利人需加强组合管理，确保证书续展及维持使用以防失效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU rules earlier rights must remain valid and enforceable at the time of the EUIPO's final decision.", "Oppositions fail if relied-upon rights lapse during proceedings, with risks persisting throughout the case.", "Rights holders must strengthen portfolio management, ensuring renewals and use maintenance to prevent invalidity."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优先选用稳定注册商标，规避短期或未注册权利的失效风险", "建立异议期权利动态监控机制，确保证书续展及使用合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize stable registered trade marks to avoid invalidity risks associated with short-term or unregistered rights.", "Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring mechanisms during opposition periods to ensure certificate renewals and use compliance.", "What to monitor: Track the legal status and validity of all earlier rights relied upon until the final EUIPO decision is issued."]
core_points_en: CJEU rules earlier rights must remain valid and enforceable at the time of the EUIPO's final decision.
Oppositions fail if relied-upon rights lapse during proceedings, with risks persisting throughout the case.
Rights holders must strengthen portfolio management, ensuring renewals and use maintenance to prevent invalidity.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize stable registered trade marks to avoid invalidity risks associated with short-term or unregistered rights.
Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring mechanisms during opposition periods to ensure certificate renewals and use compliance.
What to monitor: Track the legal status and validity of all earlier rights relied upon until the final EUIPO decision is issued.

items.title: EUIPO Report on IP-Backed Finance Makes Policy Recommendations to Unlock Up To €580 Billion in Innovation Financing
items.title_zh: EUIPO 报告建议释放高达 5800 亿欧元创新融资
items.title_en: EUIPO Report Proposes Policies to Unlock €580 Billion in IP-Backed Innovation Financing
items.summary: Today, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) published a study exploring challenges faced by EU small- and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in obtaining financing by offering intellectual property (IP) as collateral. Set against the backdrop of the EU’s recently launched Savings and Investment Union (SIU) program, the EUIPO’s study identifies several structural barriers preventing SMEs from obtaining IP-backed financing and concludes with a series of policy recommendations desig
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）发布研究报告，探讨欧盟中小企业以知识产权为抵押获取融资时面临的挑战。该研究结合欧盟储蓄与投资联盟计划，识别了阻碍知识产权融资的结构性障碍，并提出一系列政策建议，旨在缩小中小企业信贷缺口，释放高达 5800 亿欧元的经济价值。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has released a comprehensive study examining the significant challenges EU small and medium-sized enterprises face when attempting to secure financing using intellectual property as collateral. Contextualized within the framework of the EU’s newly launched Savings and Investment Union program, the report identifies critical structural barriers that currently hinder the effective utilization of IP assets for credit purposes. It concludes with a series of targeted policy recommendations aimed at bridging the persistent SME credit gap. These measures are designed to facilitate greater access to capital, potentially unlocking up to €580 billion in economic value for the broader European market by enhancing the viability of IP-backed financial instruments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应关注欧盟 IP 融资新政，评估将商标专利转化为抵押品的可行性。建议提前规范 IP 资产管理，利用潜在政策红利拓宽融资渠道，降低资金成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders operating within the European market should closely monitor evolving regulatory frameworks regarding intellectual property valuation and collateralization. As the EUIPO advances policies to integrate IP into mainstream finance, companies must assess the feasibility of converting patents and trademarks into tangible security interests. Proactive management of IP portfolios is essential to maximize eligibility for these emerging financing channels. By standardizing asset documentation and valuation practices now, businesses can better position themselves to leverage potential policy incentives. This strategic preparation helps reduce reliance on traditional equity funding, lowers overall capital costs, and enhances financial resilience through diversified access to debt markets backed by intangible assets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 发布报告分析中小企业 IP 融资难的结构障碍", "研究旨在配合欧盟储蓄与投资联盟计划解决信贷缺口", "提出政策建议以释放高达 5800 亿欧元的创新资金"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO report analyzes structural barriers preventing EU SMEs from successfully leveraging intellectual property rights as collateral for obtaining necessary business financing.", "Study aligns with the EU Savings and Investment Union initiative to address systemic credit gaps facing innovative small and medium-sized enterprises across member states.", "Policy recommendations aim to unlock approximately €580 billion in innovation financing by improving mechanisms for valuing and securing intellectual property assets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟 IP 融资政策动向，评估资产抵押可行性", "规范知识产权管理，利用新政红利拓宽融资渠道"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the feasibility of using registered trademarks and patents as collateral under new EU financing frameworks to diversify funding sources.", "Practice note: Standardize intellectual property asset management and valuation processes to ensure readiness for potential IP-backed lending opportunities.", "What to monitor: Track legislative developments stemming from the EUIPO report and the Savings and Investment Union program affecting IP collateral standards."]
core_points_en: EUIPO report analyzes structural barriers preventing EU SMEs from successfully leveraging intellectual property rights as collateral for obtaining necessary business financing.
Study aligns with the EU Savings and Investment Union initiative to address systemic credit gaps facing innovative small and medium-sized enterprises across member states.
Policy recommendations aim to unlock approximately €580 billion in innovation financing by improving mechanisms for valuing and securing intellectual property assets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate the feasibility of using registered trademarks and patents as collateral under new EU financing frameworks to diversify funding sources.
Practice note: Standardize intellectual property asset management and valuation processes to ensure readiness for potential IP-backed lending opportunities.
What to monitor: Track legislative developments stemming from the EUIPO report and the Savings and Investment Union program affecting IP collateral standards.
